# SDIRK3 decomposition fail-close matrix (PR 7C).
#
# The SDIRK3 differentiable core supports exactly ONE decomposition: a single
# MPI rank with a single tile. Every other decomposition is REFUSED before any
# communicator or halo state mutation (the guards live in advance_implicit's
# prologue in dyn_em/module_implicit_sdirk3.F). This manifest pins that
# contract; run_decomposition_matrix.sh executes it on a real WRF build.
#
# A USE_SDIRK3 binary always routes through advance_implicit, so there is no
# in-binary "stock RK3" control here — the baseline-solver comparison would
# need a separate non-USE_SDIRK3 build and is intentionally out of scope.
#
# Columns (whitespace-separated; '-' = not applicable):
#   case_id           unique label; success and reject cases never collide
#   ranks             mpirun -np value
#   nproc_x nproc_y   WRF decomposition (product MUST equal ranks)
#   mode              split | split_multitile
#                       split           = WRF_SDIRK3_SPLIT_EXPLICIT=1
#                       split_multitile = split + sdirk3_enable_ad_halo_exchange
#                                         = .true. + numtiles = 2 (AD multi-tile)
#   expect            success | reject
#   marker            exact rejection marker for reject cases; '-' for success
#
# case_id         ranks nproc_x nproc_y mode             expect  marker
np1_split         1     1       1       split            success -
np2_stage_halo    2     2       1       split            reject  SDIRK3_MPI_STAGE_HALO_UNSUPPORTED
np4_stage_halo    4     2       2       split            reject  SDIRK3_MPI_STAGE_HALO_UNSUPPORTED
np1_multitile     1     1       1       split_multitile  reject  SDIRK3_MPI_MULTI_TILE_UNSUPPORTED
